song.title: At The Name Of Jesus
song.author: Caroline M. Noel
song.lyrics: [v1] 1) At the Name of Jesus Every knee shall bow, Every tongue confess Him King of glory now; ’Tis the Father’s pleasure We should call Him Lord, Who from the beginning Was the mighty Word. [v2] 2) At His voice creation Sprang at once to sight: All the angel faces, All the hosts of light, Thrones and dominations, Stars upon their way, All the heav’nly orders, In their great array. [v3] 3) Humbled for a season, To receive a Name From the lips of sinners Unto whom He came, Faithfully He bore it, Spotless to the last, Brought it back victorious, When from death He passed; [v4] 4) Bore it up triumphant, With its human light, Through all ranks of creatures, To the central height, To the throne of Godhe,ad To the Father’s breast, Filled it with the glory Of that perfect rest. [v5] 5) Name Him, brothers, name Him, With love strong as death, But with awe and wonder, And with bated breath; He is God the Savior, He is Christ the Lord, Ever to be worshipped, Trusted, and adored. [v6] 6) In your hearts enthrone Him; There let Him subdue All that is not holy, All that is not true; Crown Him as your Captain In temptation’s hour; Let His will enfold you In its light and power. [v7] 7) Brothers, this Lord Jesus Shall return again, With His Father’s g,lory With His angel train; For all wreaths of empire Meet upon His brow, And our hearts confess Him King of glory now. [E] 1) At the Name of Jesus Every knee shall bow Every tongue confess Him King of glory now ’Tis the Father’s pleasure We should call Him Lord Who from the beginning Was the mighty Word 2) At His voice creation Sprang at once to sight All the angel faces, All the hosts of light Thrones and dominations, Stars upon their way All the heav’nly orders, In their great array 3) Humbled for a season, To receive a Name From the lips of sinners Unto whom He came Faithfully He bore it, Spotless to the last Brought it back victorious, When from death He passed 4) Bore it up triumphant, With its human light Through all ranks of creatures, To the central height To the throne of Godhead, To the Father’s breast Filled it with the glory Of that perfect rest 5) Name Him, brothers, name Him, With love strong as death But with awe and wonder, And with bated breath He is God the Savior, He is Christ the Lord Ever to be worshipped, Trusted, and adored 6) In your hearts enthrone Him; There let Him subdue All that is not holy, All that is not true Crown Him as your Captain In temptation’s hour Let His will enfold you In its light and power 7) Brothers, this Lord Jesus Shall return again With His Father’s glory, With His angel train For all wreaths of empire Meet upon His brow And our hearts confess Him King of glory now
